data.title: SunLink Health sells hospital building, related real estate of Trace Regional Hospital
data.description:
• SunLink Health Systems (NYSE:SSY) completed the sale of the hospital building and related real estate of its formerly-operated Trace Regional Hospital in Houston, Mississippi, for ~$2M.
  • The company said on Thursday the sale was carried out by the unit Southern Health Corporation of Houston and an affiliate.
  • The net proceeds of ~$1.93M will be retained for working capital and general corporate purposes.
  • A pretax loss of ~$91K is expected to be recognized in FQ2, ending Dec. 31.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Piedmont Office Realty Trust reports no damage from Hurricane Milton
data.description: • Piedmont Office Realty Trust (NYSE:PDM) said on Thursday that its property management team, after reviewing property reports for all the company’s Orlando assets, has reported virtually no damage to any of its properties from Hurricane Milton and that all personnel in the affected areas are reported safe.

data.title: SuRo Capital invests almost $55M in AI in Q3 and after
data.description:
SuRo Capital (NASDAQ:SSSS) said Q3 was one of its most active quarters in over a decade, with notable investments in AI.

During the quarter, it made a $17.5M investment in OpenAI, one of the largest artificial intelligence developers in the world, and increased its position in CoreWeave, an AI cloud computing provider, through a $5.0M secondary transaction.

Since the end of the quarter, the company made an $12.0M investment in VAST Data, an AI infrastructure platform, and increased its investment in CoreWeave with and additional $5.0M secondary.

"With these new investments and our existing investment in CW Opportunity 2 LP we have invested nearly $55.0M into some of the leading AI infrastructure companies," said SuRo Chairman and CEO Mark Klein. "Given AI’s significant addressable market, we believe dedicating a significant portion of our portfolio to AI infrastructure will prove to be successful for our shareholders."

SuRo (SSSS) stock rose 2.6% in Thursday after-hours trading.

During the quarter, it received $4.4M, net, from exits or proceeds.

SuRo Capital (SSSS) held positions in 36 portfolio companies as of Sept. 30, 2024.

The company estimated its net asset value at $6.50-$7.00 per share as of Sept. 30, 2024, compared with $6.94 at June 30, 2024.

data.title: Carlyle Secured Lending prices public offering of $300M 6.750% unsecured notes
data.description:
• Carlyle Secured Lending (NASDAQ:CGBD) has priced an underwritten public offering of $300M in aggregate principal amount of 6.750% unsecured notes due 2030.
  • The Notes will mature on Feb. 18, 2030, and may be redeemed in whole or in part at the Co.’s option at the applicable redemption price.
  • The Co. intends to use the net proceeds to repay the Co.’s outstanding debt, including the revolving credit facility, and for other general corporate purposes.
  • The offering is expected to close on Oct. 18, 2024.

data.title: GXO Logistics could see $85/share in potential takeover - analyst
data.description:
GXO Logistics (NYSE:GXO) is likely to garner a takeout multiple in the "low-to-mid-teems," a Jefferies analyst wrote after a report that the company is exploring a potential sale.

The company is working with financial advisers and reviewing its options after receiving interest from potential buyers, according to a Bloomberg report on Wednesday, which cited people with knowledge of the matter. Shares of GXO jumped 14% on Thursday.

"As we have written several times, the public markets were and are widely undervaluing GXO's highly visible and predictable long-term contract model," Jefferies analyst Stephanie Moore, who has a buy rating on GXO, wrote in a note on Thursday. "We think logical buyers would be strategics in the logistics space and think a takeout multiple could be in the low-to-mid-teens."

Potential buyers may include shipping/logistics conglomerates such as Kuehne+Nagel (OTCPK:KHNGF), Maersk (OTCPK:AMKBY) or DSV (OTCPK:DSDVF). DHL (OTCPK:DHLGY) is another possible acquirer to bolster is North American presence.

For reference, at 12x 2025 EBITDA GXO would be value at $75 a share in a takeout, Jefferies current price target, according to Moore. At 13x 2025 EBITDA GXO would be worth ~$85 a share.

data.title: S&P, Dow, Nasdaq end lower with consumer inflation, jobless claims above expectations
data.description:
Wall Street's major equity averages finished Thursday's choppy session lower after September consumer inflation and weekly jobless claims came in above estimates, data that will feed into the Federal Reserve's thinking on interest rates.

The latest economic data arrived after scorching growth in September payrolls and the Federal Reserve's big half-point rate cut at its meeting last month as it considered cooling trends in inflation and slowing in the labor market.

The S&P 500 (SP500) ended -0.2%, and the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJI) -0.1%, a day after each closed at record highs. The Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) ended lower by less than -0.1%.

“Today clearly showed what the biggest risk of the rally is. Expectations of aggressive rate cuts drove markets higher in recent months. Now, inflation came in hotter than expected, accompanied by worse-than-expected jobless claims," Leo Nelissen, part of investing group iREIT on Alpha, told Seeking Alpha.

Eight of the S&P 500's 11 sectors fell, led by Real Estate. Energy topped advancers, with oil futures (CL1:COM) (CO1:COM) up more than +4%, as Hurricane Milton tore through parts of western Florida, and traders continued to wait for Israel’s possible military response to Iran’s recent missile attack.

The latest economic data arrived after scorching growth in September payrolls and the Federal Reserve's big half-point rate cut at its meeting last month as it considered cooling trends in inflation and slowing in the labor market.

Investors on Thursday saw inflation rates rose in September, with the Bureau of Labor Statistics posting +3.3% Y/Y growth in core CPI vs. +3.2% expected, and +3.2% in August. Shelter, and motor vehicle insurance were among the upside price drivers. See a reaction roundup to the CPI report.

Meanwhile, initial jobless claims for the week ended Oct. 5 rose by 33K to 258K, vs. 231K consensus, a week when the U.S. contended with hurricanes and labor strikes.

"If inflation data continues to indicate that prices are generally rising amid a backdrop of a cooler labor market, the Fed's next meeting will undoubtedly involve a more heated discussion of which of the Fed's mandates takes precedence," Quincy Krosby, chief global strategist at LPL Financial, said in a note.

In the bond market, the 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) was up 1 basis point to 4.08%, paring its rise, while the 2-year yield (US2Y) was down 4 basis points to 3.98%.

"Although one bad month doesn’t signal stagflation, we need to be aware that the [equity] market is trading at roughly 21 times forward earnings," Nelissen said. "The room for error is slim.”

The Q3 earnings docket Friday includes reports from banks J.P. Morgan Chase (JPM) and Wells Fargo (WFC).

Broadly, "[we] expect this earnings season to produce a relief rally in US equities," Goldman Sachs said. "Elevated concerns around (1) AI spending, (2) the path of interest rates, and (3) the U.S. election have driven the (VIX) to above its long-term average and have likely been a headwind for investors looking to increase their equity allocation."

Major U.S. stock averages rose Wednesday, after the Federal Reserve's September meeting minutes said a "substantial majority" on its rate-setting board backed its big half-point cut to start an easing cycle.

On Thursday, Federal Reserve Bank of New York President John Williams said he expects policymakers to make more rate cuts as the central bank makes progress in reducing inflation.

“Based on my current forecast for the economy, I expect that it will be appropriate to continue the process of moving the stance of monetary policy to a more neutral setting over time,” Williams said in remarks for an appearance at Binghamton University.

data.title: The waiting is almost over for Tesla's watershed robotaxi event
data.description:
Tesla (NASDAQ:TSLA) closed down 1% on Thursday as traders prepped for the "We, Robot" event at 10:00 p.m. The title is a reference to Isaac Asimov’s science fiction short stories called "I, Robot." CEO Elon Musk is expected to unveil the design for a dedicated robotaxi or Cybercab, update on the FSD software package, highlight the Optimus humanoid robot, and show off either prototypes or previews of new models, potentially including the long-awaited $25K EV, an autonomous truck, or a Cybervan.

Some analysts see the event as a watershed test of whether Tesla (TSLA) deserves its premium valuation as a tech disruptor. Musk has been talking up the potential for autonomous driving for years. "The value of a fully electric autonomous fleet is generally gigantic — boggles the mind, really," he stated back in 2021. "That will be one of the most valuable things that's ever done in the history of civilization," he added. More recently, he said that Tesla (TSLA) is not a car company, but should be thought of as an AI robotics company.

Seeking Alpha Investing Group Leader Victor Dergunov thinks the introduction of a $25,000 compact EV and robotaxi fleet could significantly boost Tesla's (TSLA) market share and profitability, driving future stock price appreciation.
